response:
Emily has 24 residents interested in the workshop and divides them into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups. Each group receives 3 writing prompts initially, so 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 prompts. Additionally, she has 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among the 4 groups, which means each group receives 10 ÷ 4 = 2.5 prompts. Since prompts are indivisible, Emily will need to prepare whole prompts, so she will provide 3 extra prompts per group, totaling 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 extra prompts.

In total, Emily needs to prepare 12 initial prompts + 12 extra prompts = 24 prompts. The library of prompts will be carefully curated to ensure a variety of engaging topics for the residents. The library will also be updated regularly to keep the sessions fresh and stimulating. The END.
